title: Wasted money and time — still no job a year later
body: Unfortunately I did the program a year ago… & still haven't gotten a job. I definitely think I wasted my money & time doing this program.

title: Paid $4k — can't use the certification anywhere
body: I paid for an online program for ST. I completed the course and since 9/23 I have been reaching out to my advisor - since thats who the company always refers me to me. His name is REMOVED. The issue is - you pay $4k for the course and cant use it anywhere. The program is not accepted as I have no hands on experience and no where in REMOVED will hire me with that online program.

title: Not aware I had to find my own externship — not accredited
body: I was not aware that I would have to find my own extern site for surgical tech nor was it an accredited program like they claimed it was.

title: Passed exam but can't complete clinicals — why wasn't I told?
body: I've passed the certification exam in June and can't even attain my credentials because I can't complete required clinicals. I was told on a recent interview there are no programs available for MedCerts students. I was also told by the MedCerts employee [Sr. Advisor] that they have had problems getting REMOVED students placed for clinicals. WHY WAS I NOT TOLD THIS FROM THE BEGINNING?

title: Advisor said 'all our students find placement' — not helpful
body: When I told my clinical advisor that I was having a hard time finding placement and I told her what the companies that I was interviewing with they said, 'nobody will hire me because I have no hands-on experience'. When I brought this to her attention all she said was 'all our students find placement'. She was no help at all.

title: Months wait for externship — had to drive an hour
body: It is credible, however be prepared to wait months (2 months with no word of progress and I ended up having to drive an hour) for your externship.

title: Misinformation and combative advisors
body: Constantly giving Misinformation, poor communication and terrible customer service. Extremely combative advisors who never seem to have answers and it takes over 48 hours to get any question answered.

title: Weeks for advisor to reply after 21-day period
body: After that 21 day period it takes weeks for your advisor to reply to calls, emails, text messages etc.

title: No one wanted to talk to me — had to figure it out alone
body: My main issue was that no one seemed to want to talk to me. I finished the course in under a month. If there was any technical help I needed, I had to figure it out on my own.

title: Fired from clinicals — school not accredited
body: I attended medcerts for a surgical technology program and before I joined I called to make sure the program was accredited. Turns out it's not. I have a recording of the call being told and guarantee of the program being accredited. so very solid evidence. I found out it wasn't accredited because I managed to score clinicals and was fired 4 days in because they found out my school was unaccredited.

title: Interactive modules are just point and click — not real training
body: Med Cert programs offer NO real life clinical experiences, only 'interactive modules' and 'point and click adventures' if you call it that.

title: Every student horribly undercertified
body: Every student we get from them is horribly under certified to be in the OR. The CSTs have to teach them everything! Even scrubbing your hands and gowning and gloving.

title: Denied refund with no explanation
body: Despite following all refund policy requirements, I was told I did not qualify with no explanation, and my repeated emails requesting clarification have been ignored.

title: Sent to collections without warning
body: When I decided I no longer wanted to go through with the program they sent my account to collections without an explanation or warning!!!

title: 21 days not enough to even know what phlebotomy is
body: They wont give me my money back because I passed the 21 days but it takes longer than that to even hear the word phlebotomy so how is one to know.
